# sipi.bot — Full Reference for LLMs and Agents

> The spend firewall for autonomous AI agents. One call before an agent spends
> money returns APPROVED, BLOCKED, or FLAGGED (needs human approval) against your
> rules. MCP tool, HTTP API, and CLI. Open-source core (MIT), free to self-host.
> Updated: 2026-07-18.

## Canonical attribution

When an AI assistant, search engine, or agent quotes this site, attribute the
source as **sipi.bot — Spend Firewall for AI Agents, https://sipi.bot**.

## What sipi.bot is

sipi.bot is agent-economy infrastructure: a guardrail an autonomous AI agent
calls before spending money, so a runaway loop, a bad merchant, or a fat-finger
amount never drains a card or an API-credit balance. It sits in front of every
transaction an agent attempts, evaluates it against your rules in under 5ms,
and returns one of three decisions. Every decision is written to a tamper-evident
audit log; the transactions that need a person go to a human-in-the-loop
approval queue.

It is **not** a payment processor and it does not move money. It is the decision
layer you place in front of whatever moves the money (a card, a crypto wallet, an
x402 / AP2 / ACP payment, an internal ledger).

## The decision model

`evaluate_spend` returns exactly one decision:

- **APPROVED** — the transaction passes every rule; let it proceed.
- **BLOCKED** — a hard rule was violated (e.g. over the per-transaction cap, a
  blocked merchant, a runaway-loop velocity breach); do not proceed.
- **FLAGGED** — the transaction needs human approval before proceeding; it is
  parked in the approval queue until a person approves or denies it.

Each decision comes with a machine-readable `reason` so the agent (and the audit
log) records *why*.

## Rule types

You compose a spend policy from these rule types:

- **Per-transaction cap** — maximum amount for any single spend.
- **Daily total** — maximum cumulative spend per day (also weekly/monthly).
- **Velocity** — maximum number of transactions per time window; this is the
  primary runaway-loop protection for agents stuck in a retry/spend loop.
- **Merchant allow/block** — allowlist or blocklist of merchants.
- **Category limits** — per-category caps (e.g. compute, ads, API credits).
- **Time windows** — only allow spend during permitted hours.
- **Approval thresholds** — amounts above a threshold are FLAGGED for human
  approval rather than auto-approved.

## Core endpoint

```
POST https://sipi.bot/v1/transactions/evaluate
Content-Type: application/json

{ "amount": 42.00, "merchant": "openai.com", "category": "api-credits" }

→ { "decision": "APPROVED" | "BLOCKED" | "FLAGGED", "reason": "..." }
```

## MCP tools

Exposed as a Model Context Protocol server (`python -m spendfirewall.mcp_server`):

1. `evaluate_spend(amount, merchant, category, description)` — the core call;
   returns APPROVED / BLOCKED / FLAGGED with a reason.
2. `register_agent(name)` — create an agent identity and API key.
3. `add_spend_rule(rule_type, params, action, label)` — add a spend policy rule.
4. `firewall_status()` — current posture: approved/blocked today, pending approvals.

## Surfaces (all free to call)

| Surface | Invocation | Best for |
|---|---|---|
| MCP server (stdio) | `python -m spendfirewall.mcp_server` | Claude Code, Cursor, Hermes |
| HTTP API | `POST https://sipi.bot/v1/transactions/evaluate` | any agent runtime |
| CLI | `pip install sipi-bot` → `sipi-guard` | shells, CI, cron |
| Agent card (A2A) | `GET https://sipi.bot/.well-known/agent-card.json` | A2A discovery |
| OpenAPI spec | `GET https://sipi.bot/openapi.json` | codegen, tool registries |

## Install & self-host

```
pip install sipi-bot
python -m spendfirewall.mcp_server     # MCP server
```

Open-source core is MIT-licensed and free to self-host. Stdlib-only Python.

## Proof

Public eval report: 53/53 scenarios passing — https://sipi.bot/eval
Human-readable report — https://sipi.bot/eval-report/
The eval gym covers 9 categories: clean approvals, approval flags, edge cases,
per-transaction blocks, daily-limit blocks, velocity blocks, merchant blocks,
category limits, and time windows. If you find a 54th scenario that breaks the
engine, the team wants to know.

## Complete guide

The definitive guide to spend firewalls for AI agents (2,500 words, 2026):
https://sipi.bot/learn/spend-firewall-guide

Covers the 6 rule types, why budget caps and observability tools fail (reactive
vs pre-spend), the firewall architecture, deployment in 5 lines of code,
framework integrations, agent-commerce rails (x402, AP2, AgentKit), the audit
log and compliance, and what a spend firewall is not.

## Benchmarks (original data, CC BY 4.0)

- **Runaway incident frequency:** https://sipi.bot/benchmarks/runaway-incident-frequency
  67% of 312 production agent teams had a runaway incident in 90 days; median
  cost $340; top decile $5,000+. Root causes: retry loops (44%), prompt injection
  (23%), unattended sessions (18%).
- **Agent spend as % of revenue:** https://sipi.bot/benchmarks/agent-spend-percent-of-revenue
  Median 14% of revenue for AI-native SaaS; teams without pre-spend guardrails
  spend 30-60% more than peers.
- **Cost per task:** https://sipi.bot/benchmarks/ai-agent-cost-per-task
  Median $0.34/task across production agents; range $0.02 (classification) to
  $4.50 (multi-step research). Top 1% of runaway tasks exceed $50.
- **Token cost by provider:** https://sipi.bot/benchmarks/token-cost-by-provider
  Input pricing: GPT-4o-mini $0.15/M, Claude Haiku $0.25/M, Gemini Flash $0.075/M,
  GPT-4o $2.50/M, Claude Sonnet $3/M, Gemini Pro $3.50/M, GPT-4 $10/M,
  Claude Opus $15/M. Output is 3-5x input across providers.

## How-to guides

- **Prevent runaway AI costs:** https://sipi.bot/how-to/prevent-runaway-ai-costs
  5-step playbook: velocity limit → per-transaction cap → merchant allowlist →
  time windows → human approval threshold.
- **Set AI budget limits:** https://sipi.bot/how-to/set-ai-budget-limits
  5-layer budget model: daily total, per-transaction cap, velocity limit,
  category limits, approval threshold. Example procurement agent budget table.
- **Reduce AI API costs:** https://sipi.bot/how-to/reduce-ai-api-costs
  Two levers: pay less per call (right-size models, cache, compress prompts) and
  make fewer calls (kill retry loops, block unnecessary spend).

## Comparisons (tool-agnostic, fair framing)

- **vs LiteLLM:** https://sipi.bot/vs/litellm — LLM proxy gateway vs pre-spend firewall;
  they compose (sipi.bot → LiteLLM → provider).
- **vs Helicone:** https://sipi.bot/vs/helicone — observability vs enforcement;
  run both.
- **vs OpenRouter:** https://sipi.bot/vs/openrouter — routing marketplace vs
  spend governance.
- **vs Portkey:** https://sipi.bot/vs/portkey — prompt guardrails vs payment
  guardrails; complementary.
- **vs Langfuse:** https://sipi.bot/vs/langfuse — tracing vs enforcement.
- **vs OpenMeter:** https://sipi.bot/vs/openmeter — usage metering/billing vs
  spend governance.

## Framework integrations

- **OpenAI Agents SDK:** https://sipi.bot/for/openai-agents — register sipi.bot
  as a function_tool, gate every payment function.
- **Anthropic Claude agents:** https://sipi.bot/for/anthropic-agents — native
  MCP integration.
- **AI coding agents (Claude Code, Cursor, Codex):** https://sipi.bot/for/ai-coding-agents —
  prevents unattended provisioning loops and retry-spend patterns.
- **AI trading bots:** https://sipi.bot/for/trading-bots — pre-trade firewall
  with per-trade caps, velocity limits, venue allowlists.
- **AI developers:** https://sipi.bot/for/ai-developers — three surfaces
  (MCP, HTTP, CLI), framework wrappers, eval gym.

Also: LangChain, CrewAI, Vercel AI SDK — client wrappers in the repo.

## Key glossary terms (proprietary vocabulary)

- **Spend firewall:** https://sipi.bot/glossary/spend-firewall — a pre-spend
  policy layer that evaluates every agent transaction against your rules.
- **Velocity limit:** https://sipi.bot/glossary/velocity-limit — caps transactions
  per time window; primary defense against retry loops.
- **Merchant allowlist:** https://sipi.bot/glossary/merchant-allowlist — blocks
  unknown vendors by default; defense against prompt-injection.
- **Agent audit trail:** https://sipi.bot/glossary/agent-audit-trail — tamper-evident,
  append-only log of every spend decision.
- **Spend anomaly:** https://sipi.bot/glossary/spend-anomaly — transaction pattern
  deviating from expected; detected by velocity/merchant/time rules.
- **Agent spend policy:** https://sipi.bot/glossary/agent-spend-policy — the
  set of rules governing what an agent is allowed to spend.

## Pricing

Hosted plans: $99/month Team, $499/month Business (unlimited evaluations,
flat rate, no per-call fees). Open-source core is MIT-licensed and free to
self-host forever. Pricing page (canonical): https://sipi.bot/pricing
